briefdescription.para: 16 bytes
24 bytes
32 bytes
Output length in bytes (n).
Merkle tree height (h).
Number of subtrees (d).
Subtree height (h').
Winternitz parameter (a).
Number of layers (k).
log2(Winternitz parameter) (lgw).
Message digest length (m).
Internal SLH-DSA signature verification (FIPS 205 Algorithm 20).

para.verbatim: Gets signature and public key addresses from the instance structure.

listitem.para: Pointer to digest buffer location where TmpIdxTree value starts
Pointer to digest buffer location where TmpIdxLeaf value starts
Step 17: PK_FORS <- fors_pkFromSig(SIG_FORS, md, PK.seed, ADRS)
Pass u64 addresses for IPC compatibility
Step 18: return ht_verify(PK_FORS, SIG_HT, PK.seed, idxtree, idxleaf, PK.root)

briefdescription.para: This function verifies an SLH-DSA signature for a given message using the provided public key.

detaileddescription.para: It implements the pure SLH-DSA verification algorithm with optional context string support.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to initialized SHA instance.

Pointer to structure containing all SLH-DSA verification parameters:
DataAddr: 64-bit address of message data buffer to be verified
DataLen: Length of message data in bytes
SignatureAddr: 64-bit address of signature buffer (R || SIG_FORS || SIG_HT)
SignatureLen: Length of signature in bytes
ContextAddr: 64-bit address of optional context string buffer (can be 0)
ContextLen: Length of context string in bytes (must be <= 255)
PublicKeyAddr: 64-bit address of public key buffer (PK.seed || PK.root)
PublicKeyLen: Length of public key in bytes (must be 2n)

listitem.para: Store context length as a single byte (Algorithm 24, step 4)
Copy context string to buffer using XSecure_InByte64() for IPC compatibility
Store addresses in instance for IPC compatibility

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 *const BaseB, const u8 *const X, const u32 b, const u32 OutLen)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaBase2b

briefdescription.para: Computes the base 2^b representation of X, converts a byte string into an array of integers in the range [0, 2^b - 1].

parameterdescription.para: Output array to store the base 2^b representation.
Input byte string (must have at least (OutLen*b)/8 bytes).
The base parameter (number of bits per output element).
The number of output elements to generate.

listitem.para: Step 4: for out from 0 to out_len - 1 do
Step 13: return baseb (implicit through BaseB output parameter)

memberdef.argsstring: (const u64 SignForsAddr, const u8 *const Md, const u64 PublicKeySeedAddr, u8 *const PkFors)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaForsPkFromSig

briefdescription.para: Computes a FORS public key from a FORS signature.

detaileddescription.para: Implements Algorithm 17 (fors_pkFromSig) from FIPS 205. This function reconstructs the FORS public key using the provided signature, message digest, public seed, and address structure as specified in SLH-DSA.

Algorithm Flow for SHAKE-256s (k=22, a=14, n=32):
=== SIGNATURE STRUCTURE === Each tree signature: [secret_value (32B)] || [auth[0] (32B)] || [auth[1] (32B)] || ... || [auth[13] (32B)] Total per tree: 32 + (14 × 32) = 480 bytes
Given: BaseB[5] = 12345 (from base_2b conversion), Binary: 0b11000000111001
=== ADDRESS STRUCTURE PROGRESSION === Initial: ADRS.setTreeAddress(i), ADRS.setType(FORS_TREE)

For each j iteration: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Step 9: ADRS.setTreeHeight(j+1) │ │ Step 11/14: ADRS.setTreeIndex(calculated_parent_index) │ │ Used in: H(PK.seed, ADRS, left_input || right_input) │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Final: ADRS.setType(FORS_ROOTS) for Step 24
=== INITIAL SETUP === Tree Index (i=5): Idx = (5 << 14) + 12345 = 81920 + 12345 = 94265 NodePtr = &Data1[160], SignPtr = &SignFors[2400]
=== FORS Tree Hierarchy (for SHAKE-256s): ===

Height 0 (Leaves): 360,448 nodes (22 trees × 16,384 leaves each) Height 1: 180,224 nodes (360,448 ÷ 2) Height 2: 90,112 nodes (180,224 ÷ 2) Height 3: 45,056 nodes ... Height 13: 44 nodes Height 14 (Roots): 22 nodes (1 root per FORS tree)
Each level has half the nodes of the level below.
Tree 5 Range: Global indices 81920 to 98303

Height 0: Leaves 81920-98303 (16,384 leaves) Height 1: Parents 40960-49151 (8,192 nodes) ✓ (81920>>1 to 98303>>1) Height 2: Parents 20480-24575 (4,096 nodes) ✓ (40960>>1 to 49151>>1) ... Height 14: Root = 5 (single root node)
Leaf 94265 progression: Height 0: 94265 (leaf) Height 1: 47132 (parent) ✓ Height 2: 23566 (grandparent) Height 3: 11783 ... Height 14: 5 (Tree 5's root)
=== LEAF COMPUTATION === Step 4-6: TreeHeight=0, TreeIndex=94265 NodePtr = F(PK.seed, ADRS, SignPtr[0:31]) SignPtr advances to auth[0]

=== TREE TRAVERSAL TABLE === +-++++-++-+ | j | TreeHeight | TreeIndex | Bit | Path | Hash Operation | Alg Step | +-++++-++-+ | 0 | 1 | 47132 | 1 | ODD | auth[0] || node | Step 15 | | 1 | 2 | 23566 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[1] | Step 12 | | 2 | 3 | 11783 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[2] | Step 12 | | 3 | 4 | 5891 | 1 | ODD | auth[3] || node | Step 15 | | 4 | 5 | 2945 | 1 | ODD | auth[4] || node | Step 15 | | 5 | 6 | 1472 | 1 | ODD | auth[5] || node | Step 15 | | 6 | 7 | 736 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[6] | Step 12 | | 7 | 8 | 368 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[7] | Step 12 | | 8 | 9 | 184 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[8] | Step 12 | | 9 | 10 | 92 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[9] | Step 12 | | 10 | 11 | 46 | 0 | EVEN | node || auth[10] | Step 12 | | 11 | 12 | 23 | 1 | ODD | auth[11] || node | Step 15 | | 12 | 13 | 11 | 1 | ODD | auth[12] || node | Step 15 | | 13 | 14 | 5 | 1 | ODD | auth[13] || node | Step 15 | +-++++-++-+
=== CALCULATION FORMULAS === TreeIndex = Idx >> (j+1) = 94265 >> (j+1) Bit = (BaseB[i] >> j) & 1 = (12345 >> j) & 1
=== FINAL COMPRESSION === Step 24: PkFors = T_k(PK.seed, forspkADRS, Data1[0:703]) Input: 704 bytes (22 roots × 32 bytes) Output: 32 bytes (Final FORS public key)

=== PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS === Computational Complexity:
Hash operations: k × (1 + a) = 22 × 15 = 330 hash calls
Time complexity: O(k × a × n)

Memory Usage:
Temporary storage: 704 bytes (22 roots)
Signature input: 10,560 bytes
Working memory: Constant (reuses NodePtr)

parameterdescription.para: 64-bit address of the FORS signature data (10,560 bytes for SHAKE-256s).
Pointer to the message digest.
64-bit address of the public key seed.
Pointer to the buffer where the computed FORS public key will be stored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the public key is computed successfully.
XSECURE_SLH_DSA_FORS_COMPUTE_LEAF_ERROR if FORS leaf computation fails.
XSECURE_SLH_DSA_SIGN_VERIFY_FORS_NODE_ERROR if FORS tree node computation fails.
XSECURE_SLH_DSA_FORS_ROOT_ERROR if FORS root computation fails.
Used to represent Global lead address across all FORS Trees, range: 0 to 360,448; 0 to 16,383 for first tree
2: for i from 0 to k - 1 do
Step 6: node[0] <- F(PK.seed, ADRS, sk = current location of signature)
Step 8: for NodeIdx from 0 to a-1 do
Step 12: node[1] <- H(PK.seed, ADRS, node[0] || auth[NodeIdx])
Step 15: node[1] <- H(PK.seed, ADRS, auth[NodeIdx] || node[0])
Step 19: root[TreeIdx] <- node[0] (NodePtr points to root for this tree)

briefdescription.para: This function computes the hash of the message as required by the SLH_DSA algorithm.

para.verbatim: Gets signature and public key addresses from the instance structure for IPC compatibility.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.
For R <- SIG.getR(), R = SIG[0 : n] - access from instance address
For PK.seed [0:n] and PK.root [n:2n] - access from instance address
[in] Length of public key in bytes
M' <- toByte(0,1) || toByte(|ctx|, 1) || ctx || M, here we send all prefix data before M
M, actual data - access from instance address

memberdef.argsstring: (const u64 PublicKeyAddr, const u64 InputAddr, u8 *const Output)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaShake256sHashF

briefdescription.para: This function generates a hash value based on the provided public key and input data, and stores the result in the output buffer.

detaileddescription.para: It takes an n-byte message as input and produces an n-byte output.

parameterdescription.para: 64-bit address of the public key buffer.
64-bit address of the input data buffer to be hashed.
Pointer to the buffer where the computed hash will be stored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u64 InputAddr, const XSecure_SlhdsaChainConfig *const ChainConfig, const u64 PublicKeyAddr, u8 *const Output)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaShake256sChain

briefdescription.para: This function performs chained SLH_DSA hash operations on the input data, starting from the specified index and performing the given number of steps.

parameterdescription.para: 64-bit address of the input data buffer.

Pointer to structure containing chain configuration:
StartIdx: Index in the input buffer from where the chain operation should start
Steps: Number of hash chain steps to perform
64-bit address of the public key used for verification.
Pointer to the buffer where the resulting hash will be stored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u64 PublicKeyAddr, const u8 *const Input, u32 InputLen, u8 *const Output)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaShake256sHashTl

briefdescription.para: This function generates a hash value for the provided input data.

detaileddescription.para: The hash is computed with respect to the given public key and the result is stored in the output buffer.

parameterdescription.para: 64-bit address of the public key used for hashing.
Pointer to the input data to be hashed.
Length of the input data in bytes.
Pointer to the buffer where the computed hash will be stored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the hash was computed successfully.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u64 PublicKeyAddr, const u64 Data1Addr, const u64 Data2Addr, u8 *const Output)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaShake256sHashH

briefdescription.para: This function generates a hash value for the provided input data.

detaileddescription.para: The hash is computed with respect to the given public key and the result is stored in the output buffer.

parameterdescription.para: 64-bit address of the public key used for hashing.
64-bit address of the first input data to be hashed.
64-bit address of the second input data to be hashed.
Pointer to the buffer where the hash output will be stored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the hash was computed successfully.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *const M, const u64 SignHtAddr, const u64 PublicKeyAddr, const XSecure_SlhdsaHtIndices *const HtIndices)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaHtVerify

briefdescription.para: This function verifies a SLH-DSA hypertree signature by traversing through multiple layers of XMSS trees, starting from the bottom layer and working up to the root layer, ultimately comparing the computed root with PK.root.

detaileddescription.para:
Algorithm Flow for SHAKE-256s (d=8, h'=8, len=67, n=32):
Given: IdxTree=0x123456789ABCDEF0, IdxLeaf=0x87654321
=== HYPERTREE STRUCTURE === SLH-DSA-SHAKE-256s uses a hypertree composed of d=8 layers of XMSS trees:

Layer 7 (Top): 1 XMSS tree (contains PK.root) Layer 6: 2^8 = 256 XMSS trees Layer 5: 2^16 = 65,536 XMSS trees Layer 4: 2^24 = 16,777,216 XMSS trees Layer 3: 2^32 = 4,294,967,296 XMSS trees Layer 2: 2^40 = 1,099,511,627,776 XMSS trees Layer 1: 2^48 = 281,474,976,710,656 XMSS trees Layer 0 (Bottom): 2^56 = 72,057,594,037,927,936 XMSS trees (FORS trees connect here)
Each XMSS tree has height h'=8 (2^8 = 256 leaves)

=== TREE COUNT CALCULATION === At layer j, maximum tree index = 2^(56 - j×8) - 1
Layer 0: Max tree index = 2^56 - 1 (so 2^56 possible trees)
Layer 1: Max tree index = 2^48 - 1 (so 2^48 possible trees)
Layer 2: Max tree index = 2^40 - 1 (so 2^40 possible trees)
Layer 3: Max tree index = 2^32 - 1 (so 2^32 possible trees)
Layer 4: Max tree index = 2^24 - 1 (so 2^24 possible trees)
Layer 5: Max tree index = 2^16 - 1 (so 2^16 possible trees)
Layer 6: Max tree index = 2^8 - 1 (so 2^8 possible trees)
Layer 7: Max tree index = 2^0 - 1 = 0 (so 1 possible tree)
=== TREE DIVERSITY DEMONSTRATION === Starting with IdxTree = 0x123456789ABCDEF0:

Layer 0→1: IdxLeaf = 0x00, IdxTree = 0x123456789ABCDE00 >> 8 = 0x123456789ABCDE Layer 1→2: IdxLeaf = 0xDE, IdxTree = 0x123456789ABCDE >> 8 = 0x123456789ABC Layer 2→3: IdxLeaf = 0xBC, IdxTree = 0x123456789ABC >> 8 = 0x123456789A Layer 3→4: IdxLeaf = 0x9A, IdxTree = 0x123456789A >> 8 = 0x12345678 Layer 4→5: IdxLeaf = 0x78, IdxTree = 0x12345678 >> 8 = 0x123456 Layer 5→6: IdxLeaf = 0x56, IdxTree = 0x123456 >> 8 = 0x1234 Layer 6→7: IdxLeaf = 0x34, IdxTree = 0x1234 >> 8 = 0x12 Layer 7→END: IdxLeaf = 0x12, IdxTree = 0x12 >> 8 = 0x0 ✓

=== TRAVERSAL TABLE === +-+-+-+-++-+ | j | Layer | IdxLeaf | IdxTree | XMSS Tree Accessed | Description | +-+-+-+-++-+ | - | 0 (init) | 0x21 | 0x123456789ABCDEF0| Tree F0 at Layer 0 | Initial tree | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 1 | 1 | 0xF0 | 0x123456789ABCDE | Tree DE at Layer 1 | Parent of F0 | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 2 | 2 | 0xDE | 0x123456789ABC | Tree BC at Layer 2 | Parent of DE | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 3 | 3 | 0xBC | 0x123456789A | Tree 9A at Layer 3 | Parent of BC | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 4 | 4 | 0x9A | 0x12345678 | Tree 78 at Layer 4 | Parent of 9A | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 5 | 5 | 0x78 | 0x123456 | Tree 56 at Layer 5 | Parent of 78 | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 6 | 6 | 0x56 | 0x1234 | Tree 34 at Layer 6 | Parent of 56 | +-+-+-+-++-+ | 7 | 7 | 0x34 | 0x12 | Tree 12 at Layer 7 | Root layer | | | | | | (leaf 0x34 in tree | (final check | | | | | | 0x12) | vs PK.root) | +-+-+-+-++-+
=== CALCULATION FORMULAS === Step 6: IdxLeaf = IdxTree & ((1 << h') - 1) = IdxTree & 0xFF Step 7: IdxTree = IdxTree >> h' = IdxTree >> 8
=== DETAILED INDEX PROGRESSION === Starting with IdxTree = 0x123456789ABCDEF0:

Layer 0→1: IdxLeaf = 0xF0 & 0xFF = 0xF0 (240), IdxTree = 0x123456789ABCDEF0 >> 8 = 0x123456789ABCDE Layer 1→2: IdxLeaf = 0xDE & 0xFF = 0xDE (222), IdxTree = 0x123456789ABCDE >> 8 = 0x123456789ABC Layer 2→3: IdxLeaf = 0xBC & 0xFF = 0xBC (188), IdxTree = 0x123456789ABC >> 8 = 0x123456789A Layer 3→4: IdxLeaf = 0x9A & 0xFF = 0x9A (154), IdxTree = 0x123456789A >> 8 = 0x12345678 Layer 4→5: IdxLeaf = 0x78 & 0xFF = 0x78 (120), IdxTree = 0x12345678 >> 8 = 0x123456 Layer 5→6: IdxLeaf = 0x56 & 0xFF = 0x56 (86), IdxTree = 0x123456 >> 8 = 0x1234 Layer 6→7: IdxLeaf = 0x34 & 0xFF = 0x34 (52), IdxTree = 0x1234 >> 8 = 0x12 Layer 7→8: IdxLeaf = 0x12 & 0xFF = 0x12 (18), IdxTree = 0x12 >> 8 = 0x0

=== TREE DIVERSITY DEMONSTRATION === This example shows access to different trees at each layer:
Layer 0: Tree 0xF0 (240) out of 2^56 possible trees
Layer 1: Tree 0xDE (222) out of 2^48 possible trees
Layer 2: Tree 0xBC (188) out of 2^40 possible trees
Layer 3: Tree 0x9A (154) out of 2^32 possible trees
Layer 4: Tree 0x78 (120) out of 2^24 possible trees
Layer 5: Tree 0x56 (86) out of 2^16 possible trees
Layer 6: Tree 0x34 (52) out of 2^8 possible trees
Layer 7: Tree 0x12 (18) out of 1 possible tree (but shows non-zero until final shift)
Layer 8: Tree 0x0 (0) - The single root tree

=== XMSS VERIFICATION DETAILS === At each layer j:
ADRS.setLayerAddress(j) - Identifies current layer
ADRS.setTreeAddress(IdxTree) - Identifies specific XMSS tree
xmss_pkFromSig() computes XMSS root using:
IdxLeaf: Position within the XMSS tree
SIGtmp: XMSS signature for this layer
M: Message (FORS_PK for layer 0, previous root for others)
PK.seed: Public seed
ADRS: Address structure for domain separation

=== FINAL VERIFICATION === Step 13: Compare computed root with PK.root
Node contains final computed root (32 bytes)
PK.root = PublicKey[32:63] (second 32 bytes of public key)
Byte-by-byte comparison for constant-time verification

=== PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS === Computational Complexity:
XMSS verifications: d = 8 operations
Total hash operations: d × (len + h') = 8 × 75 = 600 hash calls
Memory usage: 2400 bytes working + 19200 bytes signature input
Time complexity: O(d × (len + h') × n)

parameterdescription.para: Message to verify (FORS public key, 32 bytes).
64-bit address of hypertree signature (19,200 bytes for SHAKE-256s).
64-bit address of public key buffer (PK.seed || PK.root, 64 bytes).
Pointer to structure containing hypertree indices (IdxTree: tree index for hypertree verification, IdxLeaf: leaf index for hypertree verification)

listitem.para: XSECURE_SLHDSA_PK_ROOT_MISMATCH_ERROR if final root comparison fails.
Step 4: node <- xmss_pkFromSig(idxleaf, SIGtmp, M, PK.seed, ADRS)
Step 5: for LevelIdx from 1 to d-1 do
Step 6: idxleaf <- idxtree mod 2^h', h' least significant bits from idxtree
Step 7: idxtree <- idxtree >> h', removing least significant h' bits from idxtree
Step 11: node <- xmss_pkFromSig(idxleaf, SIGtmp, node, PK.seed, ADRS)

briefdescription.para: This function returns the XSecure_SlhdsaInstance instance.

para.verbatim: SlhdsaInstance stores all required components for SLH-DSA signature verification.

listitem.para: SLH-DSA-SHAKE-256s (see FIPS 205 Table 2) Formula calculations: n=32, lgw=4, len1=ceil(8*32/4)=64, len2=3, len=67

briefdescription.para: This function handler calls XSecure_SlhdsaVerify server API.

parameterdescription.para: Lower 32 bit address of the XSecure_SlhdsaInputParams structure.
Higher 32 bit address of the XSecure_SlhdsaInputParams structure.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS If the SLHDSA signature verification is successful.
XST_FAILURE If there is a failure.

briefdescription.para: This function calls respective IPI handler based on the API_ID.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the command structure.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS If the handler execution is successful.
XST_INVALID_PARAM If any input parameter is invalid.
XST_FAILURE If there is a failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_ChecksumParam *const ChecksumParams, const u8 *const M, u32 *const Output)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ComputeChecksum

briefdescription.para: This function computes WOTS+ checksum and combines with base-w message representation to create the complete message array used for WOTS+ signature verification.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to checksum parameters structure containing:
Input message (n bytes, typically 32 bytes for SHAKE-256s)
Output array to store combined message and checksum digits (len1 + len2 elements, typically 67 for SHAKE-256s)

listitem.para: Buffer to hold checksum as bytes (max 4 bytes needed)
Step 2: msg <- base_2b(M, lgw, len1) - Convert message to base-w
for i from 0 to len1 - 1 do
Step 6: csum <- csum << ((8 - ((len2 * lgw) mod 8)) mod 8)
For lgw=4, this shifts left by 4 bits
Convert checksum to bytes for base_2b conversion
Step 7: msg <- msg || base_2b(toByte(csum, ceil(len2*lgw/8)), lgw, len2)
Convert checksum to base-w and append to message array

memberdef.argsstring: (u64 WotsSignAddr, u64 MAddr, u64 PublicKeyAddr, u8 *const WotsPk)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaWotsPkFromSign

briefdescription.para: This function reconstructs the WOTS+ public key from the provided WOTS+ signature and message.

detaileddescription.para: It computes the checksum, extends each signature chain to derive the public key elements, and then hashes all elements to produce the final WOTS+ public key.

Algorithm Flow for SHAKE-256s (len=67, w=16, n=32):
Given: M=FORS_PK (32 bytes), WotsSign=WOTS_signature (2144 bytes) Input message: M = [0x3A, 0x7F, 0x92, 0x1C, ...] (32 bytes)
Message chains: sig[0] to sig[63] (64 chains for 32-byte message) Checksum chains: sig[64] to sig[66] (3 chains for checksum) Total: 67 chains × 32 bytes = 2144 bytes

=== INITIAL SETUP === ADRS initialization:
Type already set to WOTS_HASH for chain computations
KeyPairAddress inherited from caller
=== STEP 1-7: MESSAGE PROCESSING AND CHECKSUM === XSecure_SlhdsaWotsCheckSum(M, Data2[]) computes:
Step 1: csum = 0 Step 2: msg = base_w(M, w, len1) - Convert message to base-16 digits

Message conversion (first 4 bytes example): M[0] = 0x3A = 0011 1010 → base_w[0] = 3, base_w[1] = 10 M[1] = 0x7F = 0111 1111 → base_w[2] = 7, base_w[3] = 15 M[2] = 0x92 = 1001 0010 → base_w[4] = 9, base_w[5] = 2 M[3] = 0x1C = 0001 1100 → base_w[6] = 1, base_w[7] = 12 ...continuing for all 32 bytes → 64 base-16 digits
Step 3-5: Compute checksum for i from 0 to len1-1: csum += w-1-msg[i] csum += (15-3) + (15-10) + (15-7) + (15-15) + (15-9) + (15-2) + ... = sum
Step 6-7: Convert checksum to base-16 and append csum_base_w = base_w(csum, w, len2) → 3 additional base-16 digits Final msg array: [msg[0]...msg[63], csum[0], csum[1], csum[2]]

=== ADDRESS PROGRESSION === For each chain i: ++++ | i | ChainAddress(i) | HW Chain Operation | ++++ | 0 | 0 | HW: chain=0, iter=3→15 | | 1 | 1 | HW: chain=1, iter=10→15 | | 2 | 2 | HW: chain=2, iter=7→15 | | ... | ... | ... | | 66 | 66 | HW: chain=66, iter=x→15 | ++++
=== FINAL PUBLIC KEY COMPUTATION === Step 12-13: Switch ADRS to WOTS_PK type XSecure_SlhdsaSetTypeClearNotKp(WOTS_PK)
Step 15: Compute final WOTS+ public key WotsPk = Tlen(PK.seed, ADRS, Data1[0:2143])

XSecure_SlhdsaShake256sHashTl computes:
Input: All 67 public key elements (Data1, 2144 bytes total)
Hash: SHAKE-256(PK.seed || ADRS || Data1[0:2143])
Output: 32-byte WOTS+ public key (WotsPk)

=== MEMORY MANAGEMENT === Buffer Usage:
Data1[2144]: Stores all 67 public key elements (67 × 32 bytes)
Data2[67]: Stores base-w representation + checksum
WotsPk[32]: Output buffer for final public key
TmpPtr: Tracks current position in signature buffer (increments by 32)

Memory Usage:
Input signature: 2144 bytes
Working buffers: 2144 + 67 = 2211 bytes
Output: 32 bytes
Total working memory: ~2.2 KB

parameterdescription.para: 64-bit address of WOTS+ signature (2144 bytes for SHAKE-256s).
64-bit address of message to verify (typically FORS public key, n bytes = 32 bytes).
64-bit address of public key seed for hash computations (n bytes = 32 bytes).
Buffer to store computed WOTS+ public key (n bytes = 32 bytes).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if WOTS+ public key computation succeeds.
Step 2: msg <- base_2b(M, lgw, len1) (handled inside WotsCheckSum)
Step 3-7: Compute checksum and append to msg (handled inside WotsCheckSum)
Step 8: for i from 0 to len-1 do
Use embedded parameter for total length instead of direct Param->len

memberdef.argsstring: (u64 IdxLeaf, u64 SignTmpAddr, u64 MAddr, u64 PublicKeyAddr, u8 *Node)
memberdef.name: XSecure_SlhdsaXmssPkFromSign

briefdescription.para: This function reconstructs the XMSS public key (tree root) using the provided XMSS signature, message, public seed, and address structure.

detaileddescription.para: It first computes the WOTS+ public key from the WOTS+ signature, then uses the authentication path to compute the tree root.

Algorithm Flow for SHAKE-256s (len=67, h'=8, n=32):
Given: IdxLeaf=58, M=FORS_PK (32 bytes), SignTmp=XMSS_signature (2400 bytes) Binary: IdxLeaf=58 = 0b00111010
WOTS+ Sig: SignTmp[0:2143] - Used to compute leaf node AUTH Path: SignTmp[2144:2399] - Used to compute tree root
=== INITIAL SETUP === Input indices: IdxLeaf = 58 (decimal) = 0b00111010 (binary)

ADRS initialization:
setTypeAndClear(WOTS_HASH) for WOTS+ computation
setKeyPairAddress(58) for leaf identification
=== WOTS+ PUBLIC KEY COMPUTATION === Step 5: node[0] = wots_pkFromSig(SignTmp[0:2143], M, PK.seed, ADRS) Result: 32-byte WOTS+ public key representing leaf 58
=== TREE TRAVERSAL SETUP === Switch ADRS to TREE type for authentication path processing SignXmssOffset = &SignTmp[2144] (points to AUTH[0])

=== XMSS TREE TRAVERSAL TABLE === +++++-+++ | k | TreeHeight | TreeIndex | Bit | Position | Hash Operation | AUTH Usage | +++++-+++ | 0 | 1 | 29 | 0 EVEN | LEFT | node || AUTH[0] | SignXmssOffset[0] | | 1 | 2 | 14 | 1 ODD | RIGHT | AUTH[1] || node | SignXmssOffset[32] | | 2 | 3 | 7 | 0 EVEN | LEFT | node || AUTH[2] | SignXmssOffset[64] | | 3 | 4 | 3 | 1 ODD | RIGHT | AUTH[3] || node | SignXmssOffset[96] | | 4 | 5 | 1 | 1 ODD | RIGHT | AUTH[4] || node | SignXmssOffset[128]| | 5 | 6 | 0 | 1 ODD | RIGHT | AUTH[5] || node | SignXmssOffset[160]| | 6 | 7 | 0 | 0 EVEN | LEFT | node || AUTH[6] | SignXmssOffset[192]| | 7 | 8 | 0 | 0 EVEN | LEFT | node || AUTH[7] | SignXmssOffset[224]| +++++-+++
=== CALCULATION FORMULAS === TreeHeight = k + 1 TreeIndex = IdxLeaf >> (k + 1) = 58 >> (k + 1) Bit = (IdxLeaf >> k) & 1 = (58 >> k) & 1
=== DETAILED BIT ANALYSIS === IdxLeaf = 58 = 0b00111010

k=0: (58 >> 0) & 1 = 0 (EVEN) → node is LEFT child, AUTH[0] is RIGHT sibling → node || AUTH[0] k=1: (58 >> 1) & 1 = 1 (ODD) → node is RIGHT child, AUTH[1] is LEFT sibling → AUTH[1] || node k=2: (58 >> 2) & 1 = 0 (EVEN) → node is LEFT child, AUTH[2] is RIGHT sibling → node || AUTH[2] k=3: (58 >> 3) & 1 = 1 (ODD) → node is RIGHT child, AUTH[3] is LEFT sibling → AUTH[3] || node k=4: (58 >> 4) & 1 = 1 (ODD) → node is RIGHT child, AUTH[4] is LEFT sibling → AUTH[4] || node k=5: (58 >> 5) & 1 = 1 (ODD) → node is RIGHT child, AUTH[5] is LEFT sibling → AUTH[5] || node k=6: (58 >> 6) & 1 = 0 (EVEN) → node is LEFT child, AUTH[6] is RIGHT sibling → node || AUTH[6] k=7: (58 >> 7) & 1 = 0 (EVEN) → node is LEFT child, AUTH[7] is RIGHT sibling → node || AUTH[7]
=== TREE STRUCTURE VISUALIZATION ===

Height 8: [ROOT] / \ Height 7: node_7 AUTH[7] / \ Height 6: node_6 AUTH[6] / \ Height 5: node_5 AUTH[5] / \ Height 4: AUTH[4] node_4 / \ Height 3: AUTH[3] node_3 / \ Height 2: AUTH[2] node_2 / \ Height 1: AUTH[1] node_1 / \ Height 0: WOTS+[58] AUTH[0] <- Leaf level (IdxLeaf=58)

Key:
WOTS+[58] is at position 58 (EVEN), so it's the LEFT child
AUTH[0] is the RIGHT sibling of WOTS+[58]
Hash: WOTS+[58] || AUTH[0] (left || right)

=== MEMORY MANAGEMENT === Node buffer usage (in-place updates):
Initial: Contains WOTS+ public key (leaf value)
k=0: Contains parent of leaf 58
k=1: Contains grandparent
...
k=7: Contains final XMSS root (32 bytes)

SignXmssOffset progression:
k=0: &SignTmp[2144] → AUTH[0]
k=1: &SignTmp[2176] → AUTH[1]
k=2: &SignTmp[2208] → AUTH[2]
...
k=7: &SignTmp[2368] → AUTH[7]

=== PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS === Computational Complexity:
Tree traversal: h' hash operations = 8 hashes
Time complexity: O(h' × n) = O(8 × 32) = O(256) per XMSS verification

Memory Usage:
Input XMSS signature: 2400 bytes total
WOTS+ signature portion: 2144 bytes
Authentication path: 256 bytes (8 × 32)
Working node buffer: 32 bytes (reused in-place)
Authentication path access: Sequential, 32 bytes per iteration
Total XMSS memory: O(signature_size + n)

parameterdescription.para: Leaf index within the XMSS tree (0 to 2^h'-1).
64-bit address of XMSS signature (2400 bytes for SHAKE-256s).
64-bit address of message to verify (typically FORS public key, 32 bytes).
64-bit address of public key seed for hash computations.
Buffer to store computed XMSS root (32 bytes).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if XMSS public key computation succeeds.
Step 1: ADRS.setTypeAndClear(WOTS_HASH) -> compute WOTS+ pk from WOTS+ sig
Step 3: sig <- SIG_XMSS.getWOTSSig() (implicit: SignTmp points to WOTS+ sig)
Step 4: AUTH <- SIG_XMSS.getXMSSAUTH() (implicit: SignXmssOffset points to AUTH)
Step 5: node[0] <- wots_pkFromSig(sig, M, PK.seed, ADRS)
Step 6: ADRS.setTypeAndClear(TREE) -> compute root from WOTS+ pk and AUTH
Step 8: for k from 0 to h' - 1 do
Step 10: if floor(idx/2^k) is odd then
Step 15: node[1] <- H(PK.seed, ADRS, AUTH[k] || node[0])
Step 12: node[1] <- H(PK.seed, ADRS, node[0] || AUTH[k])
Step 17: node[0] <- node[1] (Node already updated in-place)

briefdescription.para: Domain separation offset.
Context length offset.
Context string offset.
Maximum possible context length.
Domain separator length in bytes.
Length of ctx length field.
Domain separator for pure SLH-DSA verification.
Output length in bytes for SLH-DSA-SHAKE-256s.
Total signature length in bytes for SLH-DSA-SHAKE-256s.
Maximum length of Data1 buffer in bytes (for WOTS+ chain values)
Maximum length of Data2 buffer in bytes (for hash outputs and base conversions)
Maximum length of Data3 buffer in bytes (for single hash outputs)
Maximum length of Data4 buffer in words (for storing digits)
Number of invalid signature invocations allowed.
Length of checksum input in digits for WOTS+.
Length of checksum in digits.
Shift value for checksum calculation.
Size of checksum in bytes.
